tldr: Headlines across 10 countries frame Bill Gates' congressional testimony through two competing lenses: Epstein's blackmail of Gates (extortion/crime framing dominant in Germany, Brazil, India, USA, and Russia) and Gates' flat denial of wrongdoing before Congress (legal-accountability framing dominant in Turkey, Canada, South Africa, UK, and Southeast Asia). Sensationalist qualifiers — 'the Victim,' 'explosive testimony,' 'Painful For My Family' — are concentrated in right-populist and nationalist outlets.

evidence.quote: Claims Epstein Used His Personal Infidelities as Blackmail
Accuses Jeffrey Epstein Of Blackmail Over Extramarital
Bill Gates berichtet von Erpressung durch Epstein
Epstein usou seus casos extraconjugais contra ele
Epstein probe expands as Bill Gates and former assistant
Bill Gates rejects wrongdoing amid scrutiny over Epstein
a 'grave error in judgment,' Bill Gates says
'I never victimised anyone': Bill Gates denies wrongdoing

single_outlet_findings.finding: Only outlet to explicitly label Gates 'the Victim' in the headline — inverting the investigative framing dominant elsewhere into a sympathy/exculpatory frame where Gates is the wronged party rather than a subject of inquiry.
Only outlet to frame this as an expanding probe with multiple witnesses ('former assistant testify'), rather than centering on Gates' individual denial or Epstein's blackmail of Gates specifically.

Only outlet to add sensationalist qualifiers ('Explosive Testimony', 'Painful For My Family') absent from all other headlines, dramatising Gates' congressional account beyond straight factual reporting.

bottom_line: Headline coverage splits between a crime frame (Epstein blackmailed Gates with affair secrets) and a legal-accountability frame (Gates denies wrongdoing before Congress), with populist outlets amplifying victim and drama language absent from establishment and public-broadcaster reporting.
